data.title: Ruby AI Receptionist: 24/7 Calls & Chat Setup Guide
data.excerpt: Learn how to set up Ruby's AI receptionist to handle your calls and chats 24/7. This step-by-step tutorial shows you the easy setup process. Get started today!

$media.alt: Ruby AI Receptionist: 24/7 Calls & Chat Setup Guide

children.text: Never Miss a Lead Again: Setting Up Ruby's AI Receptionist
As a small business owner, every missed call is a missed opportunity. I used to dread stepping away from my desk, knowing a potential client might hear voicemail. Then I found
Ruby

. Ruby isn't just an answering service; it's an intelligent, blended AI and live receptionist platform that works for you 24/7. After setting it up for my consulting side hustle, my lead capture rate jumped because every call was answered professionally, instantly. If you're ready to give your business a polished, always-on front door, this guide will walk you through the process. It’s simpler than you think.
Discover how Ruby can transform your customer service
What Exactly is Ruby?
Let's get the foundation right. Ruby is a
Business Services

platform that provides a virtual receptionist service. Think of it as having a dedicated team of professional receptionists, augmented with powerful AI, who handle your inbound phone calls and live chats. The magic is in the blend: AI handles routine queries, captures caller details, and routes calls intelligently, while live, friendly agents step in for more complex conversations, scheduling, and personalized support. It’s designed specifically for the needs of small businesses, solopreneurs, and professional service firms. You can learn more about the core concept in our deep dive on
what is Ruby

. Unlike rigid auto-attendants, Ruby's agents are trained to sound like a seamless extension of your own team. I gave them specific instructions about my niche services, and callers genuinely thought they were speaking to my in-house staff.
Key Features That Make Ruby Shine
Before we dive into setup, let's look at the features that set Ruby apart. This isn't a simple answering machine; it's a full-stack communication hub.
24/7 Live Answering & AI Chat:
Your business never sleeps. Ruby’s agents are available around the clock to answer calls, and their AI-powered web chat widget can engage visitors on your site instantly, day or night.
Customized Call Handling:

You define the rules. Set custom greetings, create specific routing instructions (e.g., "Sales calls to cell, support calls to email"), and dictate how Ruby should handle different scenarios. They follow your script to a tee.
Appointment Scheduling & CRM Integration:

Ruby doesn't just take messages. They can book appointments directly into your calendar (like Calendly or Acuity) and push lead information straight into your CRM, keeping your pipeline warm. See how this works with tools like
Apollo.io
Mobile App & Real-Time Notifications:
Get instant call summaries, listen to recordings, and manage your account from their clean mobile app. You're always in the loop, even when you're not taking calls.
Bilingual Support:
They offer Spanish-speaking receptionists, which was a huge advantage for me when expanding into new markets.
These aren't just bullet points on a sales page; they're daily drivers for efficiency. The
new features and updates
they roll out consistently show they're listening to small business needs.
Your Step-by-Step Setup Guide
Ready to get started? Here’s exactly how I configured my Ruby account. It took less than 30 minutes.
Step 1: Sign Up & Choose Your Plan
Head to
Ruby's pricing page

. They offer straightforward, scalable plans based on the number of minutes or calls you expect. As a new business, I started with their introductory tier—it was perfect for my initial call volume. The process is sales-assisted, so you'll chat with a friendly expert to pick the best fit.
Step 2: The Onboarding Call

This is the most crucial step. Ruby schedules a dedicated onboarding call where you work with a specialist to build your custom call-handling instructions. Be prepared! Have your business hours, staff names, specific FAQs, and routing rules ready. The more detail you provide, the better the service. I outlined my pricing tiers and the best way to schedule a discovery call, and they nailed it every time.
Step 3: Set Up Your Forwarding

You'll configure your business phone number to forward to your unique Ruby number. This can be done via your phone carrier settings. Most modern VoIP systems (like RingCentral or Grasshopper) make this a one-click process. Now, when your phone isn't answered within a few rings, it seamlessly routes to Ruby.
Step 4: Customize Your Web Chat

If you have a website, you can add Ruby's live chat widget. You can customize the color and greeting to match your brand. The AI chatbot will engage visitors, qualify leads, and offer to connect them with a live agent if needed—directly from your site.
Step 5: Review and Refine

After your first few days, use the mobile app to review call summaries. Did the instructions work perfectly? If not, you can tweak your script anytime via your online dashboard. It's an iterative process, but Ruby makes it painless. For optimization ideas, check out our
Ruby tips and best practices
Who is Ruby Ideal For?
Ruby is a game-changer, but it's built for specific business models. From my experience and their client base, it's perfect for:
Solopreneurs & Consultants:
When you're the technician, marketer, *and* bookkeeper, Ruby handles the intake so you can focus on the work. It made me look like I had a full office staff.
Professional Services:
Law firms, accounting practices, and financial advisors use Ruby to project professionalism and ensure no sensitive client inquiry goes to voicemail.
E-commerce & Retail:
Manage order inquiries, support questions, and return requests over the phone without hiring a dedicated support team.
Healthcare & Wellness:
Clinics, therapists, and personal trainers can schedule appointments and manage patient/client calls with HIPAA-compliant processes.
Any Business Missing Calls:
If you're missing even 1-2 calls a day from potential customers, the cost of Ruby is almost certainly less than the revenue you're losing. Explore more
Ruby use cases
Pricing & Getting Started

Ruby’s pricing is transparent and designed to scale with you. Plans are based on minutes of receptionist time, with tiers for small, medium, and growing businesses. There are no long-term contracts, and you get a 14-day free trial to test the service. The value is immense. Think about it: one converted lead per month can easily cover the cost.
See Ruby's plans and start your free trial here
For a complete cost analysis and plan comparison, our guide on
why you should choose Ruby
breaks down the ROI in detail.
Final Verdict: Is Ruby Worth It?

Absolutely, without a doubt. Ruby solves a fundamental, stressful problem for small business owners: being available. The blend of intelligent AI and genuinely helpful human agents creates a service that feels premium, not robotic. It’s not just about answering calls; it’s about capturing opportunities, providing stellar first impressions, and giving you back your time and peace of mind. If you value professionalism and growth, implementing Ruby is one of the highest-ROI decisions you can make for your communication stack.
*Disclosure: We are affiliated with Ruby. If you sign up through our link, we may earn a commission at no extra cost to you.*

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)
Q: Does Ruby work with my existing business phone number?
A:
Yes! You keep your number. You simply set up call forwarding from your current phone system to the unique number Ruby assigns you. There's no need to change your business card or marketing materials.
Q: How does the AI handle complex or unusual questions?
A:

Ruby's AI is designed to handle routine queries and intake. For anything beyond that scope, or if the caller prefers, the system seamlessly transfers the call to a live, professional receptionist. The handoff is smooth and virtually unnoticeable to the caller.
Q: Is my data and my customers' data secure?
A:

Ruby takes security seriously. They are HIPAA-compliant for healthcare clients and use enterprise-grade encryption for all data, both in transit and at rest. Their live agents undergo thorough training and background checks to handle sensitive information with care.

children.text: Can Ruby receptionists schedule appointments directly into my calendar?

Yes, absolutely. Ruby integrates with popular calendar apps like Calendly, Acuity, and Google Calendar. During your onboarding, you provide them with your scheduling link or calendar access, and their agents (or AI) can book appointments in real-time while on the call with your client.
What happens if I exceed my monthly minute allowance?

Ruby plans are designed with buffer minutes and are very generous. If you consistently exceed your plan, their team will proactively reach out to help you move to a more suitable tier that matches your usage, ensuring you're always on the best plan for your business.
Do I have to sign a long-term contract?

No. Ruby operates on a month-to-month basis after the initial 14-day free trial. You can upgrade, downgrade, or cancel your service with just 30 days' notice, giving you complete flexibility as your business needs change.
